refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on achieving a specific teleoperation link availability. The phrase 'customer-defined teleoperation link availability' is kept to maintain solution neutrality and avoid specifying how availability is achieved. The FR is rephrased slightly for clarity and to emphasize the target nature of the availability metric.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or exceeds the customer-defined target (e.g., ≥ 0.999).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure continuous link availability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring and automatic failover mechanisms
Design adaptive bandwidth management to maintain link quality under varying conditions
Establish a robust error detection and correction protocol for the teleoperation link
Define clear operational policies and safety procedures to minimize link downtime
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure continuous link availability

coupling_check.notes: Redundant communication paths logically separate failure modes, supporting independence and reducing coupling between link availability and other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundancy can be implemented within cost and complexity constraints
Risk that operational policies may not fully mitigate all causes of link unavailability
Assumes that measurement of link availability is accurate and consistent over evaluation period
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time monitoring and failover as complementary DPs to enhance robustness and responsiveness beyond redundancy alone.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network and communication infrastructure components responsible for managing communication paths and ensuring link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connections, enabling redundant communication paths.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health data about communication links to other modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as redundant communication paths are core to network infrastructure; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not manage redundancy; would require significant diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on communication but does not implement redundancy; high diffusion expected.
Safety Services may react to communication failures but does not implement redundancy; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant communication paths, as it directly manages network infrastructure and communication reliability, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on external network hardware not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may require careful coordination to avoid unexpected coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant communication paths directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target by ensuring continuous link availability through redundancy, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of measured link availability ≥ 0.999.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on external network hardware not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well contained within this module. The interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are necessary but do not imply diffusion of the DP itself, as redundancy management is centralized within NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible since the NGHE Solution module manages the redundant communication paths and provides telemetry outputs for status and health data, enabling measurement of link availability against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces and ownership, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the handling of dependencies on external network hardware within the NGHE Solution module to reduce potential diffusion risks.
Establish clear interface contracts and coordination protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to avoid unexpected coupling and maintain modular integrity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition adequately addresses the functional requirement with a well-contained design parameter and implementation module. The verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the redundancy approach meets the high safety criticality acceptance criteria. Although some diffusion risks related to external network hardware dependencies exist, these are manageable through recommended revisions and do not necessitate further functional decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To clarify and document handling of dependencies on external network hardware within the NGHE Solution module and establish clear interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to reduce diffusion risks and maintain modular integrity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively manage redundant communication paths to meet availability targets.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are necessary and well-defined.
Verification methods for link availability are reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Potential dependency on external network hardware not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution module.
Possible unexpected coupling due to interface coordination with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of dependency handling on external network hardware.
Interface contracts and coordination protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
